description:
This conceptual diagram illustrates the decomposition of real 3D surface displacement (`d_real`) from Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) Line-Of-Sight (LOS) measurements. Two satellites are depicted: one in an "LOS ascending" orbit and another in an "LOS descending" orbit. Both satellites observe a single point on a curved grey surface, marked by a red dot, which is experiencing displacement.
- The red vector `d_LOS Asc` represents the displacement component measured in the ascending Line-Of-Sight direction.
- The blue vector `d_LOS Desc` represents the displacement component measured in the descending Line-Of-Sight direction.
- The black vertical vector `d_v` indicates the vertical displacement component at the surface point.
- The black dashed horizontal vector `d_WE` indicates the East-West displacement component.
- The green vector `d_real` represents the actual 3D displacement vector of the surface point. The diagram visually depicts how the `d_real` displacement can be projected onto the Line-Of-Sight vectors of the ascending and descending satellites, yielding `d_LOS Asc` and `d_LOS Desc`, and how it relates to its vertical (`d_v`) and East-West (`d_WE`) components.
